core_mechanism: mode conversion (linear scattering) between two distinct wave branches at a spatial critical point where a background flow speed crosses the local wave propagation speed, coupling otherwise-independent positive- and negative-norm modes
brief_reason: The abstract describes positive-frequency waves being converted into negative-frequency waves in a moving medium exactly where and when the flow exceeds the wave velocity, which is a wave-mode-conversion process triggered at a flow-speed = wave-speed threshold.
